headline: The pitfalls of Hungary's post-Orban transition
description: Riding a powerful wave of opposition to the outgoing government, Hungary's new prime minister, Péter Magyar, now has to balance the expectations of both left- and right-wing voters while clarifying his positions, especially regarding Ukraine.

headline: Trump says Lebanese and Israeli leaders to hold talks Thursday
Russian strikes kill 14 in Ukraine after attacks on Odesa and Kyiv
Live Nation, parent company of events giant Ticketmaster, found guilty of illegal monopoly in the US
description: The verdict could have profound repercussions in the concert world, and possible remedies could include breaking up Live Nation and Ticketmaster.

headline: Prominent Palestinian human rights advocate barred from entering France
description: Shawan Jabarin, the general director of Al-Haq, a West Bank-based NGO that is currently under US sanctions, was scheduled to speak before the European Parliament's human rights committee in Strasbourg this past Tuesday. However, French authorities denied his visa application.

headline: At Lyon's Musée des Confluences, the Loeb donation celebrates Mali's living arts
description: More than 100 masks, puppets and instruments are displayed in a way that evokes the singing, dancing and movements that once accompanied ceremonies along the Niger River.

headline: What is the controversial bill on antisemitism French MPs are debating?
description: MP Caroline Yadan has been accused of trying to silence criticism of Israel with her bill, which aims to 'combat new forms of antisemitism.' A petition against the text has gathered over 700,000 signatures.

description: Being a victim is neither a profession nor their identity. Meta, Cristina, Doris, Stéphanie, Mari and Toon live in PFAS contamination hotspots in six European countries.

headline: American Catholics outraged at Trump's attack of pope and AI-generated image of himself as Christ
description: The US president claimed, against all plausibility, that the image he posted on social media showed him as a doctor, but he was forced to take it down. The post, along with his lengthy tirade against Leo XIV, left believers stunned.

headline: Investigators denied access to Elysée Palace in events contracts probe
description: Judges ordered searches over contracts awarded to the event-planning company Shortcut Events to organize induction ceremonies at the Panthéon.
byline: Abdelhak El Idrissi and  Guillaume Daudin
